# Feature: Web Expert Agent — Inline System Prompt

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-FP-WEX-001
**Source**: Plan Feature agent (self-contained — no plan file)

Make the Web Expert Agent actually use its full system prompt at runtime. Today, [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:106](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L106) sets `WEB_EXPERT_SYSTEM_PROMPT = FALLBACK_WEB_EXPERT_PROMPT` — a ~30-line stub — and the rich 245-line [packages/arena/src/agents/prompts/web-expert-agent.md](packages/arena/src/agents/prompts/web-expert-agent.md) is never loaded. The result: every persona, expertise-domain, citation, and response-structure rule documented in the `.md` file is dead weight at runtime.

This feature establishes a **single source of truth** by inlining the full prompt directly into [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts) as a TypeScript constant and deleting the `.md` file. This unblocks all subsequent Web Expert improvements (persona overhaul, anti-hallucination guardrails, expanded commands), which depend on the prompt being live.

The chosen approach (inline) is the same approach already documented and justified in the file's own header comment at [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:10-13](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L10-L13): bundler-safe, no `readFileSync`/`__dirname` issues, no raw-loader configuration, no build-time codegen.

## 2. Technical Context

> Standing rules (TypeScript strict, naming, async/await, semicolons, Vitest+RTL) are in [.spec-lite/memory.md](.spec-lite/memory.md). Only spec-specific notes below.

### Approach

- Replace the body of the existing `FALLBACK_WEB_EXPERT_PROMPT` constant in [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts) with the **complete** content of [packages/arena/src/agents/prompts/web-expert-agent.md](packages/arena/src/agents/prompts/web-expert-agent.md), preserving every section verbatim.
- Rename the constant from `FALLBACK_WEB_EXPERT_PROMPT` → `WEB_EXPERT_SYSTEM_PROMPT_INLINE` (or similar) since it is no longer a "fallback" — it is the prompt. Collapse the redundant alias `const WEB_EXPERT_SYSTEM_PROMPT = FALLBACK_WEB_EXPERT_PROMPT;` so there is exactly one constant.
- Update the file's top-of-file JSDoc comment block ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:9-14](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L9-L14)) and the comment above the constant ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:62-73](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L62-L73)) to reflect inlining as the chosen design (not a fallback).
- Delete [packages/arena/src/agents/prompts/web-expert-agent.md](packages/arena/src/agents/prompts/web-expert-agent.md) once the inline content is verified equivalent.
- Use a TypeScript template literal (backtick string). Internal backticks within the prompt (one occurs inside `\`http\`` in the existing fallback) must be escaped consistently throughout — verify all occurrences.

### Spec-Specific Overrides

No overrides — see memory.

### Out of Scope

- Editing the *content* of the prompt itself. Persona/audience-tier changes ship in F2; anti-hallucination guardrails ship in F3. This feature only relocates the existing `.md` content into TypeScript verbatim.
- Any changes to `wave-client-agent.md` (the sibling prompt for the other agent). Only the web-expert prompt is in scope.
- Changes to the public agent API or graph wiring.

## 3. Data Model (Granular)

N/A — no data model changes.

## 4. Files

- `packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts` — replace `FALLBACK_WEB_EXPERT_PROMPT` body with full inlined prompt; rename constant; remove the redundant alias; update file-header and constant-header comments.
- `packages/arena/src/agents/prompts/web-expert-agent.md` — **delete** (per memory: "Always ask for user confirmation before deleting any file. Verify it is not imported elsewhere before proceeding." — this is handled in TASK-001's verification step).
- `packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts` — add a test asserting the inlined prompt is the active one and exposes the expected sections.

## 5. Dependencies

None. This is the foundational feature — F2/F3/F4/F5 depend on it.

## 6. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Verify the `.md` file is not imported anywhere else, then delete it

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Search the entire repo for references to `web-expert-agent.md` (any path form: `prompts/web-expert-agent.md`, `./web-expert-agent.md`, etc.) using project-wide grep.
  - Search for any `readFileSync` / `import.*\.md` / webpack `raw-loader` / Vite `?raw` reference that could load the file at build or runtime.
  - If any reference is found outside of plain documentation links (e.g., the existing TS comment at [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:63](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L63)), STOP and report to the user before proceeding.
  - Ask the user to confirm deletion (per memory General §1).
  - On confirmation, delete `packages/arena/src/agents/prompts/web-expert-agent.md`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: N/A (deletion verified by TASK-002's test that the inlined prompt is the active source).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: None — the `.md` file is internal; no external docs reference it.
- **Verify**:
  - `grep -r "web-expert-agent.md" packages/ scripts/ webpack.config.* vite.config.*` returns zero hits after deletion.
  - `pnpm --filter @wave-client/arena build` succeeds.
  - `pnpm --filter @wave-client/arena test` passes.

### TASK-002: Inline the full prompt into `webExpertAgent.ts` and consolidate the constant

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts), replace the body of the existing `FALLBACK_WEB_EXPERT_PROMPT` template literal ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:74-103](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L74-L103)) with the **complete, verbatim** content of the previous `prompts/web-expert-agent.md` (every section: Identity, Expertise Domains, Reference Sources, Citation Rules, Response Structure, Web Fetcher Integration, Behavioral Rules, Commands).
  - Rename the constant `FALLBACK_WEB_EXPERT_PROMPT` → `WEB_EXPERT_SYSTEM_PROMPT_INLINE`.
  - Delete the now-redundant alias line `const WEB_EXPERT_SYSTEM_PROMPT = FALLBACK_WEB_EXPERT_PROMPT;` ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:106](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L106)) and replace usages of `WEB_EXPERT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` (single use at [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:123](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L123)) with `WEB_EXPERT_SYSTEM_PROMPT_INLINE`.
  - Update the file-header JSDoc ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:9-14](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L9-L14)) to state that the prompt is inlined as the canonical source (no companion `.md`).
  - Update the JSDoc above the constant ([packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:62-73](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L62-L73)) to remove "fallback" language and describe it as the canonical inlined prompt.
  - Escape all backticks within the prompt content correctly for the template literal (the previous `.md` uses `\`http\``, fenced code blocks with `\`\`\``, and inline backticks for header names — every backtick must be `\`` in TS).
  - Preserve markdown structure (headings, tables, blockquotes, fenced blocks) exactly as in the `.md` source — only the carrier changes.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: covered in TASK-003.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**:
  - Update the file-header JSDoc as noted above.
  - Update the constant-header JSDoc as noted above.
  - No README/CHANGELOG entry required — this is an internal refactor with no behavior change for end users (memory Documentation §3 applies to user-facing/API changes).
- **Verify**:
  - `pnpm --filter @wave-client/arena build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors.
  - Manually diff the inlined string against the deleted `.md` content (one-time check during implementation) to confirm no section was dropped or reordered.

### TASK-003: Add a unit test asserting the inlined prompt is wired through to the LLM

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In [packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts](packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts), add a `describe('inlined system prompt', ...)` block.
  - **Test 1 — prompt wiring**: invoke `chat()` with a trivial user message; capture the messages passed to the mock LLM's `invoke`; assert the first message is a `SystemMessage` whose `.content` contains a marker substring that exists *only* in the full inlined prompt and *not* in the previous truncated fallback (e.g., `'Reference Sources'`, `'Tier 1 — Specifications & Standards'`, or `'webauthn.guide'`). Pick a string that appears nowhere else in the file.
  - **Test 2 — `systemPrompt` override still wins**: pass `systemPrompt: 'CUSTOM_PROMPT_FOR_TEST'` to `createWebExpertAgent`, run `chat()`, assert the captured `SystemMessage.content === 'CUSTOM_PROMPT_FOR_TEST'`. This guards the existing override path at [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts:121-123](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts#L121-L123).
  - Reuse the existing `makeMockLLM()` and `fetchSpy` setup already in the file — do not introduce new mocking infrastructure.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: this task *is* the test task. Two cases listed above. The Unit Test sub-agent can later expand with: assertion that every documented `## ` section header from the prompt is present (loop over a list of expected headings); assertion that no internal markdown link in the prompt is malformed.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none — test code is self-documenting via the `it()` description.
- **Verify**:
  - `pnpm --filter @wave-client/arena test webExpertAgent` passes.
  - Both new test cases appear in the Vitest output and are green.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002.

## 7. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A — no auth surface.
- **Error Handling**: N/A — no new fallible operations. The existing `Result`-pattern usage in the agent is untouched.
- **Logging**: N/A — no new log statements. Existing `console.info` calls in [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts) are unchanged.
- **Security**: The inlined prompt is static text shipped with the bundle; it contains no secrets and is identical in trust level to the previously inlined fallback.
- **Performance**: The prompt grows from ~30 lines to ~245 lines (~10 KB). This is a one-time bundle-size addition (negligible vs. LangGraph + LangChain) and a one-time per-LLM-call token-cost addition. No runtime hot-path impact.
- **Backwards compatibility**: Per memory General §3, not required. The `systemPrompt` config override remains the public escape hatch.
